title: Aureline extension-host capability-world, host-negotiation, and compatibility-bridge vocabulary (ADR seed)
description: Seed boundary schema for the capability-world row, the host-negotiation packet, the world-admission decision record, and the compatibility-bridge profile row reserved in docs/adr/0019-wasm-wit-extension-host-and-capability-worlds.md. This is an ADR-seed schema: the vocabulary and field set are pinned so the extension-runtime, SDK, registry-mirror, install-review, and permission-inspector lanes at a later milestone do not invent extension-host-shaped fields ad hoc. The WIT files at wit/aureline/*.wit are the normative source of truth for the capability-world interfaces themselves; the capability-world registry at artifacts/extensions/capability_worlds.yaml binds worlds to host families, lifecycle rows, and permission-scope projections; this schema is the cross-tool boundary every non-owning surface reads at this milestone. Adding a new member to any enumerated set, a new world slug, or an additive field is additive-minor with a host_negotiation_schema_version bump; repurposing an existing member is breaking and requires a new decision row. Raw Wasm component bytes, raw core module bytes, raw external-host launch bodies, raw helper-binary invocation bodies, raw bridge-shim payloads, raw signing-key material, and raw policy-bundle bytes MUST NOT appear in any record; records carry refs only.

host_negotiation_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this host-negotiation vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

world_vocabulary_version.description: Integer vocabulary version for the capability-world slug enumeration and the host-negotiation vocabulary. Bumped additive-minor when a new world slug, a new budget class, a new narrowing reason, a new unsupported-world reason, a new audit event id, or a new denial reason is added.

record_kind.description: Discriminator for the concrete payload shape. Every record carries exactly one kind.

opaque_id.description: Opaque, stable id safe to log, safe on RPC, safe in manifests / journal entries / support bundles / claim manifests.

monotonic_timestamp.description: ISO 8601 UTC timestamp produced from a monotonic clock source.

world_identity.description: Capability-world identity under the reserved aureline namespace. Pattern: aureline:<slug>@<MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH>. Third-party worlds MUST NOT mint identities under this namespace; a separate schema version will admit third-party identities once the successor ADR lands.

world_slug.description: Frozen capability-world slug vocabulary reserved by the ADR seed. Additional slugs are additive-minor with a world_vocabulary_version bump.

world_semver.description: Semantic version for a capability world (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH). Minor bumps are additive; major bumps are breaking and require a new decision row.

host_contract_family.description: Frozen host-contract-family vocabulary inherited from docs/adr/0012-extension-manifest-permission-publisher-policy.md. Binding shape per family is reserved in artifacts/extensions/capability_worlds.yaml.

permission_scope_kind.description: Frozen permission-scope vocabulary inherited from docs/adr/0012-extension-manifest-permission-publisher-policy.md. Additional kinds are additive-minor with a permission_vocabulary_version bump.

budget_declaration.description: Declared budget classes for one negotiation. A class that is not declared is treated as 'host default'; the successor ADR binds exact defaults. Policy packs and trust state MAY narrow any class further; widening is denied.

reason_label.description: Human-legible reason label for the budget declaration. Raw secret material forbidden.

source_reason_refs.description: Refs to the source rows that produced the narrowing: policy-pack constraint rows, ADR-0011 dependency markers, ADR-0018 trust-decision records, or ADR-0012 host-contract binding rows. Raw bundle bytes never included.

reason_label.description: Human-legible narrowing label. Raw secret material forbidden.

repair_affordance_label.description: Human-legible repair affordance (e.g. 'grant trust', 'lift admin deny-list', 'upgrade extension ABI'). Rendered on install-review and permission-inspector surfaces; silent fallback to a generic 'unavailable' chip is forbidden.

host_negotiation_audit_event_id.description: Frozen host-negotiation audit-event-id vocabulary reserved by the ADR seed. Additional ids are additive-minor.

host_negotiation_denial_reason.description: Frozen host-negotiation denial-reason vocabulary reserved by the ADR seed. These narrow ADR-0012's existing denial set for host-negotiation failures; silent downgrade to a generic 'unavailable' chip is forbidden.

trust_state_gating_posture.description: Per-world trust-state gating posture. admitted_in_restricted worlds MAY run under ADR-0018 restricted states but remain read-only and side-effect free; blocked_in_restricted worlds MUST NOT admit any capability under restricted states.

client_scope_default.description: Default client scope for the world row. Projects onto the ADR-0011 client-scope axis. Remote-scoped worlds require the remote agent's identity-mode envelope at negotiation time.

retirement_status.description: Retirement status for a capability-world row. Retired slugs are never reused; the successor world (if any) is named in successor_world_ref.

bridge_translation_class.description: Frozen compatibility-bridge translation-class vocabulary reserved by the ADR seed. The concrete profile set ships in the successor ADR.

bridge_world_disposition.description: Per-world disposition under a compatibility-bridge profile. 'honoured' exposes the world verbatim; 'translated' maps a foreign surface onto the world; 'refused' denies with compatibility_bridge_required_not_present.

wit_package_ref.description: Repo-relative path to the WIT seed for this world. Canonical source of truth for the interface.

summary.description: Short reviewer-facing summary of the world.

supported_host_families.description: Host families that MAY surface this world. Declaring a family outside the ADR-0012 enum is non-conforming.

permission_scope_projection.description: Permission-scope set the world projects onto at admission time. Every member MUST come from the ADR-0012 permission-scope vocabulary.

capability_lifecycle_row_ref.description: Ref to the ADR-0011 capability-lifecycle row for this world. Projects the five-axis readiness / support / channel / freshness / client-scope markers.

extension_version.description: SemVer string the manifest declared at negotiation time.

declared_capability_worlds.description: Ordered list of capability-world identities the manifest declared.

offered_capability_worlds.description: Ordered list of capability-world identities the host is prepared to surface for this session. MAY be narrower than declared.

negotiated_capability_worlds.description: Ordered list of capability-world identities admitted for this session. Always a subset of both declared and offered; widening is denied.

guest_abi_range.description: Declared guest-side ABI range (carried by the manifest).

permission_vocabulary_version.description: ADR-0012 permission-vocabulary version used to project capability scopes.

narrowing_reasons.description: One entry per declared world that was not admitted. Hosts that silently drop a declaration are non-conforming.

unsupported_world_decisions.description: One entry per declared world the host does not implement at this ABI range, retired, or refused by a bridge.

trust_state_ref.description: Ref to the ADR-0018 trust_decision_record gating the negotiation.

identity_mode_ref.description: Ref to the ADR-0001 identity-mode envelope.

execution_context_ref.description: Ref to the ADR-0009 execution-context id when any admitted world binds to the shell / task surface. Null otherwise.

policy_pack_constraint_refs.description: Ordered list of ADR-0012 policy-pack constraint rows that narrowed the negotiation.

compatibility_bridge_binding_ref.description: Ref to the compatibility-bridge binding row when host_contract_family = compatibility_bridge. Null otherwise.

audit_event_refs.description: Ordered list of host-negotiation audit events emitted for this negotiation.

redaction_class.description: Redaction-class label; MUST match the ADR-0012 redaction_class vocabulary.

allOf.description: Compatibility-bridge binding ref is required when the host family is compatibility_bridge.

Negotiated worlds MUST be a non-widening subset of declared worlds; declared-but-not-negotiated worlds MUST resolve to either a narrowing_reasons entry or an unsupported_world_decisions entry. Enforcement is host-side; this schema pins the shape so downstream surfaces can rely on one of the two arrays being present when negotiated worlds diverge from declared worlds.

admission_outcome.description: Per-world admission outcome. 'admitted' means the world entered the negotiated set intact; 'narrowed' means a capability inside the world was narrowed (budget, permission-scope projection, or client scope); 'denied' means the world did not enter the negotiated set.

narrowing_reason_refs.description: Refs to narrowing-reason entries in the parent host-negotiation packet.

unsupported_world_decision_ref.description: Ref to an unsupported_world_decision_entry when admission_outcome = 'denied' for an unsupported-world reason.

repair_affordance_label.description: Human-legible repair affordance rendered to review surfaces.

honoured_worlds.description: Worlds the bridge exposes to the foreign extension verbatim.

translated_worlds.description: Worlds the bridge exposes under a translation layer. The bridge MAY NOT widen the world; translation is surface-only.

refused_worlds.description: Worlds the bridge does not expose. A manifest declaring one denies with compatibility_bridge_required_not_present (ADR-0012) or host_negotiation_compatibility_bridge_refused_world (this seed).

foreign_budget_label.description: Opaque label for a foreign budget axis the bridge translates from.

aureline_budget_class.description: Aureline budget class the foreign axis translates to. MUST be one of the enumerated classes in this schema; repurposing is breaking.
